definition: I2S intercepts a single ICC colorspace or device-class FOURCC equality/switch CMP, logs the exact 4-byte constant, and substitutes it at one header offset, directly hitting a specific switch arm or equality result.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the switch comparison against cmsSigXYZData (0x58595A20), finds the matching 'XYZ ' bytes in the input at offset 0x10, and substitutes the logged 'Lab ' comparand, flipping PCS from XYZ to Lab to reach the false arm.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input profile PCS field at offset 0x10 must spell 'Lab ' (not 'XYZ'/'RGB') so InPCS != cmsSigXYZData and the false branch is taken

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ICC profile's connection-space signature at byte offset 0x10-0x13 must NOT equal the literal 'XYZ ' (cmsSigXYZData = 0x58595A20). When it spells 'Lab ' (0x4C616220) or another non-XYZ space, the switch on InPCS at line 429 falls through cmsSigXYZData and InPCS takes the false side of the case test, routing into the cmsSigLabData / default arms.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winning-resolving seeds carry a non-XYZ PCS literal at 0x10: seed 1/3/5 (cmplog) and seed 2 hold 'Lab' / 'MCH4Lab', and the BYTE DIFF shows W bytes 4c(L)/61(a)/62(b) at offsets 0x10/0x11/0x12 versus L bytes 52(R)/47(G)/42(B). The W per-role overlay reaches the cmsSigLabData arm (lines 454-477) that only executes on the false side of the XYZ case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 'RGB XYZ' at 0x10-0x17, so InPCS resolves to cmsSigXYZData and the true side of line 429 is taken (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L T=60 F=0 at line 429). The loser never reaches the Lab-input arm and the false branch stays globally blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted profile-signature CMP (the switch on InPCS compiles to integer comparisons against the cmsSigXYZData constant 0x58595A20), finds the matching 'XYZ ' bytes in the input, and substitutes the other observed operand ('Lab '/etc.) at offset 0x10, directly flipping the PCS field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reliably synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC at that offset; value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback gives no monotone path to a discrete FOURCC literal, so both losers stall.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked false side at line 429 is the XYZ-input case of a switch on InPCS, so the false side requires InPCS != cmsSigXYZData.

Winners carry a non-XYZ PCS literal 'Lab' at the profile-signature offset.
At offsets 0x10-0x12 winning bytes are L/a/b while losing bytes are R/G/B, the discriminating PCS field.
Loser seeds hold 'RGB XYZ' at the PCS field, making InPCS = cmsSigXYZData (true side).
Losers take only the true side of line 429 and never the false side.

analysis.summary_one_line: ICC profile colorspace signature field must equal the 4-byte FOURCC 'CMYK' at offset 0x10 to take the 4-channel case arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature in the ICC profile header at offsets 0x10-0x13 must hold the exact magic value 'CMYK' (43 4D 59 4B). cmsChannelsOfColorSpace switches on this signature; only cmsSigCmykData (='CMYK') reaches case line 901, the blocked true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries bytes 43 4D 59 4B ('CMYK') at offsets 0x10-0x13; the BYTE DIFF shows offset 0x10 is 43(C) for all 22 winner samples while losers are dominated by 52(R). This exact FOURCC drives the switch to the case cmsSigCmykData arm.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offsets 0x10-0x13, i.e. cmsSigRgbData, which dispatches to the 3-channel arm (line 893) and never reaches line 901; the L overlay only ever hits the default/return-3 arms.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (cmplog and value_profile_cmplog) intercepts the integer/string CMP that compares the parsed colorspace signature against the cmsSigCmykData literal, logs the constant, and substitutes 'CMYK' into the input bytes. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must stumble onto the exact 4-byte FOURCC by chance, which they fail to do (loser blocked in 9-10/10 trials).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the cmsSigCmykData case arm of cmsChannelsOfColorSpace, a switch on the colorspace signature, with the true side globally blocked.
The switch dispatches on ColorSpace and only the Cmyk/MCH4/4color arm returns 4 channels, distinct from the 3-channel RGB arm.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal CMYK FOURCC at offset 0x10.
Loser-blocking seeds carry RGB (not CMYK) at the same colorspace-signature offsets.
Offset 0x10 is uniformly 43(C) across all winner samples but dominated by 52(R) in losers, marking it as the decisive dataflow byte.
Offset 0x13 (the 4th FOURCC byte) is uniformly 4B(K) in winners but space/other in losers, confirming the full CMYK token boundary.
On the call chain, the blocker true side has W hits but L is 0/100 true, while losers explore only the default arm.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 901  T=72 F=70  T=0 F=100  case cmsSigCmykData:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other CMP operand at those offsets.

`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one

evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (the loser of pair 1) has no I2SRandReplace mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte 'CMYK' FOURCC equality where naive/value_profile resolve the branch at the same rate as cmplog/value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage) would refute the I2S-magic-FOURCC attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S signal is a lower bound and shared ancestor 5441efb6193a1a03 appears in both chains at shallow depth, so the exact mutation that first inserted 'CMYK' is not directly named, only inferred from the I2S-floor heuristic.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the CMP of the parsed signature against cmsSigMCHFData and substitutes the 'MCHF' literal at the matching input offset; a TokenReplace lineage signal confirms I2S ancestry in the winner.
signature.one_line: I2S places 'MCHF' FOURCC to clear colorspace switch-case gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Profile color-space signature at offset 0x10 must equal the exact 4-byte FOURCC 'MCHF' to take the case arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4 bytes at input offsets 0x10-0x13 must equal the literal FOURCC 'MCHF' (4D 43 48 46), the cmsSigMCHFData color-space signature parsed from the profile header.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All cmplog winner-resolving seeds carry MCHF at offset 0x10-0x13 (4D 43 48 46), and one was produced by TokenReplace placing the MCHF token verbatim.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser-blocking seed has 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13 instead, so the switch falls to a different/default arm and the MCHF case stays blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer/string CMP of the parsed signature against cmsSigMCHFData, logs both operands, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant 'MCHF' at the matching input offset; naive has no such substitution and must hit the 4-byte literal by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the cmsSigMCHFData case arm of the color-space switch.
The enclosing function switches on the parsed ColorSpace signature.
Winner seeds carry MCHF at offset 0x10-0x13 while losers carry RGB+space there.
Offset 0x13 differs: winners have F (0x46), losers have a space (0x20).
A cmplog winner-resolving seed has MCHF in its bytes and was mutated by TokenReplace.
A cmplog winner seed was produced solely by TokenReplace.
A value_profile winner-resolving seed carries MCHF.
naive loser-blocking seeds carry RGB+space at offset 0x10.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.

scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive blocked the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog and value_profile resolved it.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch arm requires the exact 4-byte signature 'MCHF' at a fixed offset shows naive resolving it as fast as cmplog/value_profile (no cmplog or value_profile advantage), refuting that the 4-byte-literal gate is what blocks naive.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile gradient claim is inferred from mechanism description plus one winner seed; no per-CMP_MAP-bucket distance trace is shown proving prefix-distance climbing on this specific signature compare.
